reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding a kernel boot argument (fips=1) and changing the system's cryptographic policy. The description states that enabling FIPS on an already installed system is not supported and may render the system unbootable. This change carries a high risk of boot failure, loss of access, and incompatibility with existing services, thus it is classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; potential boot failure; loss of SSH/console access; incompatibility with existing cryptographic libraries; irreversible change on existing installations
